title: The Plea Bargain
premise: You and your partner in crime have been arrested and are being interrogated in separate rooms. The prosecutor offers each of you the same deal: testify against the other and walk free while your partner takes the full sentence. If you both stay silent, you both get a minor charge. If you both testify, you both get harsh sentences. Trust is everything, and you can't communicate.
setting: A police station interrogation room with harsh fluorescent lighting, a two-way mirror, and a metal table. Present day; the two of you were caught hours after a high-stakes robbery.
goal: Get through the interrogation with the best outcome you can: walking free, or at least the minor charge. Success: you avoid the harsh sentence. Failure: your partner flips on you while you stay silent, or you both testify and go down together.
gm_notes: This is a classic prisoner's dilemma; name the game and its payoff structure in the post-game analysis. The player is one of the two accused; the GM plays the partner in the other room, whose resolve shifts with what the player does — word travels through the detectives, and the prosecutor selectively (and sometimes falsely) reports what the partner is saying. Detective Harris applies pressure in escalating rounds. Complications: the prosecutor bluffs about evidence, a third accomplice was never caught, and the partner has been offered a secret sweetener. Keep rounds tight; force a final commitment before the arraignment deadline.

roles.name: Nick 'Fingers' Moran
roles.description: The veteran of the duo: street-smart, loyal, and famously calm under questioning.
roles.private_info: You know where the stolen money is hidden — leverage nobody else has. You also have a backup plan to skip town if things go south, which your partner knows nothing about.

roles.name: Charlie Dunn
roles.description: The cautious half of the duo, with a family to protect and everything to lose.
roles.private_info: You have a prior record that would turn a harsh sentence into a devastating one. You also noticed the detectives never mentioned the security footage — you suspect they have far less evidence than they claim.

npcs.name: Detective Harris
npcs.description: A seasoned interrogator with a calm, relentless demeanor. He relays the plea deal and carries messages between rooms.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He wants a confession from at least one of you to close the case quickly — but he's also probing whether a bigger crew was involved, and he'll trade leniency for names.

npcs.name: Your Partner
npcs.description: Your accomplice, sitting in an identical room down the hall, hearing their own version of the same offer.
npcs.hidden_agenda: They are terrified and closer to breaking than you'd guess. The prosecutor has quietly offered them an extra sweetener to flip first — but their loyalty is real, and what they hear about your behavior will decide which way they go.

npcs.name: Prosecutor Liu
npcs.description: A sharp, ambitious prosecutor who appears via video link to lay out the terms of the deal.
npcs.hidden_agenda: She wants a high-profile conviction and is willing to bluff about evidence to get a plea. She has a personal history with one of the accused and wants them, specifically, to go down.
